content:
URGENT EXPO GO CONNECTIVITY AND IVX FULL APP QA

Current Expo URL:

exp://169.254.0.21:8083

This URL is not accepted as a reliable LAN development address.

169.254.x.x is a link-local/APIPA address and may not be reachable from the
Android phone. Audit the complete network, Metro, Expo Router, bundle, owner
authentication, API, and application runtime now.

Do not create a new project.
Do not replace the existing IVX application.
Do not reset the repository.
Do not switch the app to localhost.
Do not add Rork runtime dependencies.
Do not report success because Metro starts.
The real IVX application must open and function inside Expo Go.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — CONFIRM THE REAL PROJECT
==================================================

Verify:

PROJECT_ROOT=
PACKAGE_JSON=
APP_CONFIG=
EXPO_ROUTER_ENTRY=
GITHUB_REPOSITORY=
GITHUB_BRANCH=
GITHUB_HEAD_SHA=
APP_NAME=
ANDROID_PACKAGE=
IOS_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER=

Confirm this is the existing IVX application with all historical routes and
modules.

Do not run create-expo-app.
Do not initialize a replacement project.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — DIAGNOSE THE 169.254 ADDRESS
==================================================

Inspect all network interfaces on the development machine.

Return:

INTERFACE
STATUS
IP_ADDRESS
SUBNET_MASK
GATEWAY
NETWORK_TYPE
REACHABLE_FROM_PHONE
SELECTED_BY_EXPO

Identify why Expo selected:

169.254.0.21

Check for:

- computer not connected to Wi-Fi;
- Ethernet disconnected;
- DHCP failure;
- phone hotspot adapter;
- USB tethering adapter;
- virtual machine adapter;
- Docker adapter;
- VPN adapter;
- WSL adapter;
- inactive network interface;
- wrong interface priority;
- router client isolation;
- guest Wi-Fi isolation;
- firewall blocking Metro;
- phone using cellular instead of Wi-Fi.

Required correction:

Use a normal reachable private LAN address such as:

192.168.x.x
10.x.x.x
172.16.x.x through 172.31.x.x

Do not advertise a 169.254.x.x address unless direct device reachability has
been proven.

==================================================
PHASE 3 — VERIFY PHONE AND COMPUTER NETWORK
==================================================

Confirm:

1. Android phone and development computer are connected to the same Wi-Fi.
2. Phone is not using a guest network.
3. Mobile data is temporarily disabled for testing.
4. VPN is disabled on phone and computer.
5. Private DNS/ad blocker is disabled for testing.
6. Router client/AP isolation is disabled.
7. Computer firewall allows Node/Expo/Metro.
8. Port 8083 is listening on all interfaces.
9. Phone can reach the computer IP.
10. No proxy is rewriting the request.

Return:

COMPUTER_LAN_IP=
PHONE_LAN_IP=
SAME_SUBNET=true/false
PORT_8083_LISTENING=
PHONE_TO_COMPUTER_REACHABLE=
FIREWALL_RESULT=
VPN_RESULT=
CLIENT_ISOLATION_RESULT=

==================================================
PHASE 4 — START EXPO CORRECTLY
==================================================

Stop every stale Expo or Metro process first.

Identify processes using:

- 8081
- 8082
- 8083
- 19000
- 19001
- 19002

Terminate only stale project processes.

Then start from the correct IVX Expo directory with cache cleared.

Use the project equivalent of:

cd expo
bun x expo start --clear --lan --port 8083

or:

npx expo start --clear --lan --port 8083

Verify Metro binds to:

0.0.0.0:8083

and advertises the correct reachable LAN IP.

Return:

WORKING_DIRECTORY=
METRO_PID=
METRO_BIND_ADDRESS=
METRO_PORT=
ADVERTISED_EXPO_URL=
QR_CODE_GENERATED=
BUNDLE_ENDPOINT_STATUS=

The final LAN URL must not use 169.254.x.x unless the Android device can
actually reach it.

==================================================
PHASE 5 — PROVIDE TUNNEL FALLBACK
==================================================

If LAN mode cannot be reached because of router or firewall restrictions,
start Tunnel mode:

bun x expo start --clear --tunnel

or the equivalent Expo CLI command.

Return:

TUNNEL_URL=
TUNNEL_STATUS=
PHONE_OPEN_RESULT=

Tunnel mode is an approved development fallback when the device cannot reach
the LAN development server.

Do not change production API URLs when using the tunnel.

==================================================
PHASE 6 — VERIFY EXPO ROUTER CONFIGURATION
==================================================

Audit:

- package.json main entry;
- expo-router package version;
- app directory;
- route root;
- metro.config.js;
- babel configuration;
- app.json/app.config;
- plugins;
- typed routes;
- route discovery.

Confirm:

1. `main` uses the correct Expo Router entry.
2. Metro extends standard Expo configuration.
3. No obsolete Rork Metro wrapper remains.
4. Context modules are enabled.
5. The correct route directory is loaded.
6. No empty fallback application is being bundled.
7. No alternate `/index` entry bypasses Expo Router.
8. No stale bundle points to another project.

Return:

ROUTER_ENTRY=
ROUTE_DIRECTORY=
ROUTES_DISCOVERED=
METRO_CONFIG_STATUS=
BABEL_STATUS=
APP_CONFIG_STATUS=
STALE_ENTRY_FOUND=
FINAL_STATUS=

Expo Router requires compatible Metro configuration; custom Metro
configuration can prevent route context modules from loading correctly.
Use standard Expo/Expo Router configuration. 

==================================================
PHASE 7 — VERIFY THE BUNDLE IS THE REAL IVX APP
==================================================

Do not accept bundle success alone.

Inspect the served bundle and prove it contains:

- IVX Holdings identity;
- current route registry;
- Owner Login;
- Admin HQ;
- Access Control;
- Members;
- Investors;
- Buyers;
- JV Deals;
- Influencers;
- Realtors;
- Brokers;
- Tokenized Investors;
- IVX Staff;
- CRM;
- Properties;
- Chat;
- IVX Owner AI;
- Developer Workspace;
- Variables;
- Security Box.

Return:

BUNDLE_SIZE=
BUNDLE_HASH=
ROUTE_COUNT=
EXPECTED_MARKERS_FOUND=
STALE_MARKERS_FOUND=
WRONG_PROJECT_MARKERS_FOUND=
STATUS=

==================================================
PHASE 8 — VERIFY PRODUCTION API CONFIGURATION
==================================================

Expo Go is only the development runtime.

The app must still use production services:

API_BASE_URL=https://api.ivxholding.com
WEB_URL=https://ivxholding.com

Audit all API configuration sources:

- app.config;
- app.json;
- environment files;
- Expo public variables;
- API clients;
- fetch wrappers;
- Axios clients;
- hardcoded URLs;
- fallback URLs.

Remove production-reachable use of:

- localhost
- 127.0.0.1
- 10.0.2.2
- old Render URLs
- Rork URLs
- stale tunnels
- local fallback APIs

Return:

API_BASE_URL=
HEALTH_STATUS=
VERSION_STATUS=
AUTH_ENDPOINT_STATUS=
CHAT_ENDPOINT_STATUS=
MEMBERS_ENDPOINT_STATUS=
ACCESS_CONTROL_STATUS=

==================================================
PHASE 9 — VERIFY SUPABASE CONFIGURATION
==================================================

Confirm Expo Go loads the correct public Supabase configuration:

- production Supabase URL;
- production anon key;
- correct project reference;
- auth redirect handling;
- session persistence.

Never bundle:

- service-role key;
- database password;
- GitHub token;
- Render API key;
- AI provider secret.

Return:

SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF=
PUBLIC_URL_LOADED=
ANON_KEY_LOADED=
SERVICE_ROLE_IN_CLIENT=false
AUTH_INITIALIZED=
SESSION_RESTORE_RESULT=

==================================================
PHASE 10 — OWNER LOGIN QA IN EXPO GO
==================================================

Run a fresh owner login in the actual Expo Go application.

Test:

1. Open IVX in Expo Go.
2. Sign out any stale session.
3. Sign in as the approved owner.
4. Confirm Supabase returns a live session.
5. Confirm access token exists.
6. Confirm refresh token exists.
7. Confirm owner email allowlist passes.
8. Open Profile.
9. Open Admin HQ.
10. Open Access Control.
11. Open Variables.
12. Open Security Box.
13. Open Developer Workspace.
14. Open IVX Owner AI.
15. Close Expo Go.
16. Reopen Expo Go.
17. Confirm session restoration.
18. Confirm no false `tokenPresent=false` banner.
19. Confirm no Expo owner-proof gate blocks the real owner.
20. Confirm owner-only API calls return authorized results.

Return:

OWNER_LOGIN=
TOKEN_PRESENT=
REFRESH_TOKEN_PRESENT=
OWNER_ALLOWLISTED=
ADMIN_HQ=
ACCESS_CONTROL=
VARIABLES=
DEVELOPER_WORKSPACE=
OWNER_AI=
RESTART_SESSION=
STATUS=

==================================================
PHASE 11 — FULL EXPO GO SCREEN QA
==================================================

Open and verify every critical screen in Expo Go:

CORE

- Home
- Invest
- Market
- Portfolio
- Chat
- Profile
- Notifications
- Settings

OWNER

- Owner Login
- Admin Panel
- Admin HQ
- Owner Console
- Access Control
- Members
- Investors
- Buyers
- JV Deals
- Influencers
- Realtors
- Brokers
- Tokenized Investors
- IVX Staff
- Variables
- Security Box
- Developer Workspace
- Deployment Control
- Audit Trail

BUSINESS

- CRM
- Properties
- Deals
- Transactions
- Wallet
- Revenue
- Fees
- Landing Control
- Registration Audit
- Email
- Media
- Analytics

For each screen return:

SCREEN
ROUTE
OPENED
RENDERED
API_CALLED
HTTP_STATUS
DATA_SOURCE
ERROR
STATUS

Allowed statuses:

- VERIFIED_WORKING
- UI_WORKS_API_FAILS
- AUTH_BLOCKED
- ROUTE_FAILS
- CRASHES
- NOT_IMPLEMENTED

Fix every critical screen that is not VERIFIED_WORKING.

==================================================
PHASE 12 — IVX OWNER AI QA
==================================================

From Expo Go:

1. Sign in as owner.
2. Send a normal message.
3. Receive a real AI response.
4. Verify no fake deterministic response is presented as the LLM result.
5. Verify permanent user message ID.
6. Verify permanent AI message ID.
7. Verify conversation ID.
8. Reload Chat.
9. Confirm persistence.
10. Restart Expo Go.
11. Confirm messages remain.
12. Send 20 sequential messages.
13. Verify no duplicates.
14. Verify correct ordering.
15. Test Retry.
16. Test image.
17. Test PDF.
18. Test voice.
19. Confirm no Rork AI Cloud call.
20. Confirm production API is used.

==================================================
PHASE 13 — NETWORK FAILURE HANDLING
==================================================

Test:

- Wi-Fi disconnect;
- Wi-Fi reconnect;
- phone switches between cellular and Wi-Fi;
- Metro temporarily stops;
- API temporarily unavailable;
- app background/foreground;
- Expo Go restart.

The app must:

- show honest connection errors;
- not lose saved messages;
- not duplicate requests;
- recover after reconnect;
- distinguish Metro failure from production API failure.

==================================================
PHASE 14 — CACHE AND STALE BUNDLE REMOVAL
==================================================

If the app shows an old build:

1. Stop Metro.
2. Clear Expo/Metro cache.
3. Remove project `.expo` cache only if safe.
4. Restart with `--clear`.
5. Close Expo Go completely.
6. Remove only the cached project entry if required.
7. Re-scan the current QR code.
8. Verify the current build marker and commit SHA.

Do not clear production database data.
Do not delete owner credentials from the server vault.

==================================================
PHASE 15 — FINAL EVIDENCE
==================================================

Return:

NETWORK

- computer IP
- phone IP
- same subnet
- Metro bind address
- Metro port
- LAN URL
- tunnel URL if used
- connectivity result

PROJECT

- repository
- branch
- commit SHA
- project root
- app config
- route count
- bundle hash
- bundle size

EXPO

- Expo CLI version
- Expo SDK version
- Expo Go compatible
- Metro status
- Router status
- cache-cleared status
- QR code result

PRODUCTION

- API base URL
- health HTTP status
- backend version
- Supabase project
- owner auth
- chat
- members
- Access Control

SCREEN QA

- total critical screens
- verified
- failed
- blocked
- crashes

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT
==================================================

Return exactly one:

EXPO GO IVX APP VERIFIED WORKING

or

EXPO GO IVX APP NOT VERIFIED

Do not return VERIFIED unless:

- the Android phone reaches Metro;
- the URL uses a reachable address or tunnel;
- the real IVX bundle loads;
- Expo Router loads all expected routes;
- production API is used;
- owner login works;
- owner session restores;
- Admin HQ opens;
- Access Control opens;
- IVX Owner AI works;
- critical screens pass;
- no Rork runtime dependency is used;
- evidence is complete.

If not verified, return only:

FAILED_LAYER:
NETWORK:
METRO:
EXPO_ROUTER:
BUNDLE:
AUTH:
API:
SCREEN:
ERROR:
ROOT_CAUSE:
FIX_APPLIED:
RETEST_RESULT:
OWNER_ACTION_REQUIRED:
